steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The task is to “emphasize how the two works are similar.” The correct answer must explicitly highlight something shared by both ‘Thin in the Old’ and ‘Victor’, not contrast them or present facts about only one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Composed by artist Jean-Michel Basquiat in 1986 and 1987, respectively, both *Thin in the Old* and *Victor* use acrylic paint as a medium.: Uses “both” and identifies acrylic as a shared medium across the two works — directly emphasizes similarity. ✓
  • Choice B — Artist Jean-Michel Basquiat completed *Thin in the Old* in 1986, and *Victor* followed in 1987.: States completion dates for each work separately. Noting sequential dates does not highlight what the two works share. ✗
  • Choice C — Artist Jean-Michel Basquiat's work *Thin in the Old* is composed of acrylic, oil, and paper collage, while *Victor* is composed of acrylic, oil stick, and crayon.: Uses “while” to contrast the two compositions. Though both share acrylic, this sentence foregrounds differences, not similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — Between artist Jean-Michel Basquiat's works *Victor* and *Thin in the Old*, the former is the larger of the two.: Highlights a difference in size between the works, not a similarity. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
